mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 0.11%) continues to compress the equity risk premium relative to bonds, keeping capital flowing into equities and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the rate stays below 0.5%.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during the ZIRP era.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in 3-month average nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth relative to the trailing 6-month average signals softening labor demand, which markets interpret as a leading indicator of decelerating corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS monthly change falls below 70% of the trailing 6-month average change
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median 20-day forward SPX returns, or hit rate of negative returns is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional base rate (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects synchronized strength in consumer and residential investment demand, a combination that historically precedes broad-based earnings upgrades and positive equity momentum over the next month; neither series alone reliably predicts this effect.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=437006M and HOUST=1065K as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show positive 3-month momentum simultaneously, forward 20-day SPX returns are not significantly higher than periods when only one or neither series accelerates (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and output, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces modest positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO month-over-month prints are not significantly different from returns following flat/negative prints (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime combined with subdued inflation (CPIAUCSL growth below trend), the market prices an extended period of accommodative policy, supporting valuation multiple expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this mechanism weakens once inflation accelerates and rate hike expectations build.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with low inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns turn negative or flat (median <= 0) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%, contradicting the predicted positive drift.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) crosses below 5.5%, approaching estimates of the natural rate, markets anticipate accelerated wage pressure and earlier Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as discount rate expectations rise.
regime_hint: labor market tightening near full employment
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following UNRATE crossing below 5.5% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or no statistically significant negative mean return is observed (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-recovery regime, sustained increases in housing starts (HOUST) over multiple quarters signal durable consumer confidence and construction-driven GDP contribution, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is specific to early-to-mid recovery phases and does not hold in late-cycle or housing-bust regimes.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive association with rising HOUST trend during recovery-labeled periods (rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of declining industrial production (INDPRO) signal an emerging manufacturing contraction that precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets price in reduced corporate output and earnings risk.
failure_condition: Following triggered 3-month INDPRO declines, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive or non-negative in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the predicted negative drift.
